rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FUMIKO TAKAGI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/17/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. Although file stamped on 10/22/24, the amended petition was not actually e-filed until 10/23/24, when it was first available to be viewed on the Court's website. [LRSFF 14.14 A.] (2) The 13100 affidavit filed on 8/28/24 does not appear to have a certified copy of Toshiko Fukushima's death certificate attached to it. (3) The summary of account in paragraph 11 of the amended petition does not balance. (4) There are still no proper schedules attached to the "accounting" (such as it is). A check register is not adequate. Each schedule must support each separate category on the Summary of Account: receipts, disbursements, gains or losses on sale, etc. Deadline is 12/3/24 to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. If an amendment to the amended petition is filed or a second fully amended petition is filed, please do NOT mail notice until the amending pleading is e-filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALICIA P. ALBAHAE REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST UTD 07/16/2022
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Title To Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although it would have been more persuasive if the deceased trustor's pour-over will had been attached to the petition. Paragraph 11 mentions that formal estate proceedings would have been necessary to transfer accounts, but there were no accounts, and formal proceedings without a will would have resulted in an intestate estate proceeding.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MELANIE LOK SEPARATE PROPERTY REVOCABLE TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 6, 2017 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order 1)Returning Property Wrongfully Held By The Trust; 2)Order For Distribution Of Trust Property Owing To Petitioner; And 3)Imposition
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/24/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The petitioner is reportedly the guardian of the minor children, but there is no evidence of a formal guardianship, and no guardian ad litem appears to have appointed yet... which appears to be at an impasse. (4) There appears to be a cyclical dependency problem: without a copy of the trust, the Court cannot know its terms or who is entitled to notice, etc. (5) A copy of the SNT (or a certification of that SNT) should be filed to confirm that the petitioner is the trustee of a valid SNT. After the procedural issues are addressed (proof of service of notice with remote instructions, submission of a proposed order, supplement to the petition with a copy of the SNT or certification of the SNT), an appearance will likely be required even if no formal response or no copy of the trust has been filed. Deadline to deliver the proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any new filed papers is 12/10/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

[LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The petitioner is reportedly the guardian of the minor children, but there is no evidence of a formal guardianship, and no guardian ad litem appears to have appointed yet... which appears to be at an impasse. (4) There appears to be a cyclical dependency problem: without a copy of the trust, the Court cannot know its terms or who is entitled to notice, etc. After the procedural issues are addressed (proof of service of notice with remote instructions and submission of a proposed order), an appearance will likely be required even if no formal response or no copy of the trust has been filed. Deadline to deliver the proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any new filed papers is 12/10/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
